title: The WORST Parts About UCONN - University of Connecticut - Campus Interviews - LTU
body: YES! The wind and the cold are terrible. And it's a big campus, so it can be a long cold walk to class. I'd say the worst problem, as an alum and CT resident, is that it just seems like the administration is more concerned about how the school ranks in the listing of best colleges than they are about the students. Over $1B has been spent building new buildings etc over the last 20 years, but they just keep tacking on tuition and fee increases every year to pay for it. When I went there it looked like a cement block prison. Now it looks like a luxury resort. Which is great if you want to attract full price international students, but not so great if you're a middle class  in-state student.
author: @gidget123456

title: The WORST Parts About UCONN - University of Connecticut - Campus Interviews - LTU
body: UCONN is more diverse than I thought. CT is 70% white and UCONN is 15% lower than that. If there was an issue of diversity I feel the college would have a lower concentration of poc than the State but it's actually the opposite.
author: @ljaggerl

title: The WORST Parts About UCONN - University of Connecticut - Campus Interviews - LTU

75-79 , towers, school of engineering.

Carpe diem.

title: The WORST Parts About UCONN - University of Connecticut - Campus Interviews - LTU
body: We toured UConn , loved the vibe but after touring other schools, we observed lawn chairs to study outside, eatery with a patio and umbrellas and multiple outside picnic tables , tables to study outside. title: The WORST Parts About UCONN - University of Connecticut - Campus Interviews - LTU
body:
2:25 when your entire life revolves around portraying yourself as a victim.

Even though 80% of Connecticut is white, "about 45% of UConn’s Class of 2024 are students of color —18% are Hispanic/Latinx and 7.5% are Black. Of Connecticut’s 169 towns and cities, 162 are represented in the new class. A large number of transfer students come from Connecticut community colleges and other in-state institutions, the university said.

At the Storrs campus, where about 3,900 freshmen are enrolled, about 42% of first-year students are ethnic and racial minorities, and about 14% are international students. Of the first-year students at Storrs, 51% are female and 49% are male." Hartford Courant

title: UConn STUNS Duke with 0.4 seconds left in Elite Eight instant classic | Full finish
body:
Duke looked like the clear better team for most of this game, but UConn just kept chipping away, and chipping away, until the final seconds when they made a GREAT defensive play, followed by one of the most clutch 3 point game winners in March Madness history.

UConn's resilience won this game.

title: Azzi Fudd Is Going To UCONN! Paige Bueckers & Azzi's CRAZY Quarantine Day In The Life
body:
UCONN HUSKIES are destine for a few more championships. Stay healthy ladies.

My daughter played basketball in school here in San Antonio and made all city among many girls who went to D1 schools so in my mind the competition was some of the toughest and throughout the state there were ladies getting signed by Baylor, OK, SMU, UCONN and many many others. During my daughters play my wife and I were there to help these AAU teams she was on, raising money, cleaning bathrooms (for the tournaments - I'd been to a few tournaments where the bathrooms were nasty so my wife and I volunteered to make sure that the girls and the guests at least had a clean bathroom).

When I saw the short pieces of the team getting together at the hotel, for dinner, on the court and saw the coaches being silly it reminded me of my experience was and will always be remember with people who's color is just a tad darker than my own. (I'm half Asian and half Hispanic with a lost background of European) We always had fun. BBQ'ing, going to the store to pick up drinks for the girls, gathering around the hotel pool with families, those were fine beautiful memories.

Thanks for finding that file in the back of my mind.
author: @Elc0chin0

title: All Access: Inside Dan Hurley's intense UConn practice
body: Thank you again Graham. You’re the best at this in our present day. In my opinion.  There’s a lot of great interviewers but you have a way about you and you get the interviews we want to see. Well done
author: @scatman8403

title: All Access: Inside Dan Hurley's intense UConn practice
body:
great interview graham. really enjoyed getting an in-depth look at how coach hurley runs his program. i like how you have the full interview and then broken down into segments of interest.

really appreciate your hard work.
cheers from southern ontario, canada 🍁
author: @carparthero

title: All Access: Inside Dan Hurley's intense UConn practice
body: Coach Hurley is an amazing coach. I think that goes without saying. I’ve been coached by guys like this and (admittedly) it rubbed off on me. 😂🤷🏿The problem with these videos is there is a 6th grade basketball coach who hasn’t played since he was in junior high who is gonna see this and be like “that is how I am gonna coach my team”. 🤦🏿🤦🏿 There are very few players today (outside of the top college programs) who respond well to this style of coaching)
author: @sigma71999

title: All Access: Inside Dan Hurley's intense UConn practice
body: As a fan of UCONN I am very comfortable with our coaching staff. I have confidence in them that they will get the most out of every player.
author: @butter7734

title: All Access: Inside Dan Hurley's intense UConn practice
body: Accountability,,from the bottom to the top !!  I have always respected a hard boss because they brought the best out of me.
author: @oakleyjack7600

title: PAIGE BUECKERS | Day In The Life! "She's Already A MONSTER" 😱
body: I didn’t even know she was injured! Hope her recovery goes well. She makes the women’s game fun to watch.
author: @nyelle.1

title: PAIGE BUECKERS | Day In The Life! "She's Already A MONSTER" 😱
body: Ppl be like " shes so overrated " and shes actually out there beating other teams with an injured ankle LOL
author: @treeone12

title: PAIGE BUECKERS | Day In The Life! "She's Already A MONSTER" 😱
body: I loved every second of this vid. Love seeing how Paige is doing. No question in my mind that she is going to come back hungry and ready. Keep up the rehab girl.
author: @briansmith3764

title: PAIGE BUECKERS | Day In The Life! "She's Already A MONSTER" 😱
body: Paige is doing a great job with a great attitude. The elite group is doing a great job to support and rehab Paige. You see the recovery process working. It's great and amazing. God is blessing her for her faithfulness and passion for the game. Adversity should make the best in us. Her test will be her testimony and she will teach others who go thru the test you can do all things through Christ which strengthen her.
author: @daviddiaz2007

title: PAIGE BUECKERS | Day In The Life! "She's Already A MONSTER" 😱
body: I'm inspired because I'm in a boot in because I broke my ankle so I'm working on my strength in my leg
author: @_thedensnzz

title: PAIGE BUECKERS | Day In The Life! "She's Already A MONSTER" 😱
body: People don’t recognize that excellence is the product of incredible hard work behind the scenes. It’s that way with Paige. It’s that way with the UConn team.
author: @mrmojorisin8752
